title: Stable Cursor Timelines, Filters, Reverse Navigation, and Reflow · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A disability-led research network incrementally builds stable accessible entry timeline from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and browser fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, HTTP-client, Express, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, async-cleanup, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of stable accessible entry timeline while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Choose a documented total order with unique tie-breaker and state which edits can move an entry.
Runtime-validate version, tenant, direction, sort values, filters, expiry, and integrity without granting authority or query control.
Implement forward and reverse keyset predicates aligned with sort, null policy, index, limit-plus-one, and output reversal.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Deterministic Sort
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 3859 during a PostgreSQL lease and migration fault exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove deterministic ordering, signed cursors, keyset directions, changing data, and accessible navigation; every external value must remain unknown until current runtime validation admits it; then change one tenant, cursor, reader command, authorization, validator, sanitizer, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Deterministic Sort.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose a documented total order with unique tie-breaker and state which edits can move an entry. For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Deterministic Sort, keep Feed Ts Deterministic Sort connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
